distillationMethod.pipeline: RIA-TV++ adapted to a single executable skill because the user requested one output skill directory
distillationMethod.stage0: Adler-style whole-book understanding captured in BOOK_OVERVIEW.md
distillationMethod.stage1: Five candidate extraction files created under candidates/: frameworks, principles, cases, counter-examples, glossary
distillationMethod.stage1_5: Triple verification applied: V1 cross-chapter support, V2 predictive power for new standards questions, V3 non-triviality beyond generic advice
distillationMethod.stage2: RIA++ skill construction with Reading, Interpretation, Past Application, Future Trigger, Workflow, Failure Modes, and Boundaries
distillationMethod.stage3: Index and related-skill links captured in INDEX.md and SKILL.md
distillationMethod.stage4: Darwin-compatible trigger, non-trigger, and edge-case prompts captured in test-prompts.json

keyConcepts: make it run before you make it faster

skillLinterView.triggerPrecision: pass after adding an explicit non-trigger sentence for mature compliance, generic history, biography, and project-management requests
skillLinterView.executionSteps: pass after adding operating contract, input record, three gates, hard stops, scorecard, and decision rule
skillLinterView.boundariesFailureModesOutput: pass after adding independent-use boundary, compatibility contract output, risk scorecard, and source trace requirement

skillComplyView.testPromptCoverage: pass after expanding should_trigger, should_not_trigger, and edge_cases to cover standards law, de facto adoption, migration, compatibility, architecture/components split, optimization maturity, and future-system warning signs
skillComplyView.independentCapability: pass: ordinary application no longer requires reading the source book; local sources are reserved for trace, contradiction resolution, or audit

runtimeDependencyPolicy: Closed skill: execute from SKILL.md; use references/source-notes.md only as local optional provenance; do not require external source folders, webpages, original books, source reports, machine-local paths, or cross-skill files.
